page: Microsoft Azure E96s_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Esv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The configuration includes 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs via hyperthreading, supported by a 260 MB L3 cache. Memory capacity is 768.0 GB, yielding an 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio. The instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier memory bandwidth, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 7372.61 GB/sec and large block uncached write speeds at 209.25 GB/sec. Multi-core processing is highly efficient, as demonstrated by a top-tier Geekbench multi-core score and a Passmark CPU score of 97473.82. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and bzip3 single-threaded decompression scoring in the average tier. However, multi-threaded operations, such as bzip3 decompression and software compilation, rank in the top 10% of tested systems.

With its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and strong multi-threaded capabilities, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads. It demonstrates top-tier performance in database operations, static web serving throughput, and Redis SET operations, which reached 137.56 million operations per second. It also shows strong capability in large-model LLM inference, processing prompts for a 70B model at 41.06 tokens per second. The absence of local storage means external storage costs must be factored in, but the high resource density of 768 GB RAM paired with 96 dedicated vCPUs provides qualitative cost efficiency for large-scale enterprise databases and memory-bound analytical applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E96s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Esv6 family, powered by a dedicated 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores. It features 768.0 GB of RAM, a 260 MB L3 cache, and lacks local storage or integrated GPUs. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier memory bandwidth, with uncached read speeds of 509.22 GB/sec and write speeds of 209.25 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is exceptionally strong, as evidenced by top-tier results in Passmark CPU tests, software compilation, and multi-threaded decompression. Conversely, single-core benchmarks fall into the average tier. This hardware profile is highly efficient for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, including large-scale database operations, Redis deployments, and large-model LLM inference prompt processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96s_v6 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor. It delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96s_v6 is a memory-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, built for database and memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring ninety-six virtual cores, high-bandwidth memory, and dedicated processing for enterprise database and analytical workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores
768.0 GB of RAM providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-threaded processing capabilities
No local storage included, requiring external Premium Storage attachment
Optimized for large-scale database operations, Redis, and LLM inference
